steps.work: A rational number is any number that can be written as a/b, where a and b are integers and b ≠ 0.
steps.explanation: The word 'rational' comes from 'ratio.' Fractions, integers, terminating decimals, and repeating decimals are all rational.

steps.instruction: Can -7 be written as a fraction of integers?
steps.explanation: Every integer is rational because it can be written over 1.

steps.instruction: Can 0.333... (repeating) be written as a fraction?
steps.explanation: Repeating decimals are always rational. They can be converted to fractions.

title: Placing a Rational Number on the Number Line

steps.work: -3/4 is a negative fraction. It is between -1 and 0.
steps.explanation: Since -3/4 is negative, it is to the left of 0. Since its absolute value (3/4) is less than 1, it is between -1 and 0.

steps.work: Divide the segment from -1 to 0 into 4 equal parts. -3/4 is at the third mark from 0 going left.
steps.explanation: The denominator (4) tells us how many equal parts. The numerator (3) tells us how many parts from 0.

steps.instruction: Convert to a decimal to double-check.
steps.work: -3/4 = -0.75. This is 3/4 of the way from 0 to -1, confirming our placement.
steps.explanation: Converting to decimal gives another way to verify position. -0.75 is indeed between -1 and 0, closer to -1.
